name: blescope
summary: Sniff and decode BLE GATT traffic, fingerprint device profiles, and assert on insecure pairing/characteristics in CI against a capture.

documentation.readme: Sniff and decode BLE GATT traffic, fingerprint device profiles, and assert on insecure pairing/characteristics in CI against a capture.
usage_sections: Features: - ✅ Decode GATT service/characteristic UUIDs (16-bit + Bluetooth-base 128-bit) - ✅ Load captures from JSON or a tolerant key: value text form (and stdin) - ✅ Fingerprint device profile (smartlock · fitnesstracker · beacon · hidperipheral · environmentalsensor) - ✅ Audit SMP pairi

arguments.name: --authorized
arguments.description: confirm you own / are permitted to test the target devices (REQUIRED)

arguments.name: -blescope
arguments.description: run: blescope scan captures/device.json --format sarif > blescope.sarif

arguments.name: -connect
arguments.description: pip install "

arguments.name: --ddg
arguments.description: use DuckDuckGo HTML scrape

arguments.name: --feed
arguments.description: fetch an RSS/Atom feed URL instead of searching

arguments.name: --format
arguments.description: output format (default: table)

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Key: Value

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: -hostable
arguments.description: point it at a target, get prioritized results in the format your workflow already speaks (table · JSON · SARIF), gate CI on it, and let agents drive it over MCP.

arguments.name: -in
arguments.description: * **OFF by default.** Active code never runs from the passive ``scan`` path.

arguments.name: -IOCAP
arguments.description: NoInputNoOutput I/O capability forces Just Works

arguments.name: -JUSTWORKS
arguments.description: Just Works pairing (no MITM protection)

arguments.name: -LEGACY
arguments.description: LE Legacy Pairing (no Secure Connections)

arguments.name: -live
arguments.description: pull live GATT from authorized in-scope BLE

arguments.name: -PLAINTEXT-CTRL
arguments.description: Plaintext write to control characteristic 2a56

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: maximum active probes per second (default: 1.0)

arguments.name: -scanning
arguments.description: ```bash

arguments.name: -UNAUTH-WRITE
arguments.description: Unauthenticated writable control characteristic 2a56

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: show program's version number and exit

arguments.name: -WEAKKEY
arguments.description: Short encryption key (7 bytes)

arguments.name: --when
arguments.description: recency bound for search (e.g. 1d, 7d, 1h)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ukBaz/python-bluezero (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with mackowiakd/BLE-Security-PoC (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with hbldh/bleak (score=11); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: Sniff and decode BLE GATT traffic, fingerprint device profiles, and assert on insecure pairing/characteristics in CI against a capture.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Sniff and decode BLE GATT traffic, fingerprint device profiles, and assert on insecure pairing/characteristics in CI against a capture.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install cognis-blescope; pip install -e .            # or: pipx install blescope. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `blescope scan capture.json    # → prioritized findings in seconds (passive, offline)`. Further invocations from upstream: `blescope-emit --version`; `blescope 0.6.0`. Sniff and decode BLE GATT traffic, fingerprint device profiles, and assert on insecure pairing/characteristics in CI against a capture.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Bluetooth, use blescope because Sniff and decode BLE GATT traffic, fingerprint device profiles, and assert on insecure pairing/characteristics in CI against a capture.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `blescope scan capture.json    # → prioritized findings in seconds (passive, offline)` as documented upstream for blescope.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `blescope-emit --version` as documented upstream for blescope.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `blescope 0.6.0` as documented upstream for blescope.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `blescope-emit --help` as documented upstream for blescope.
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair blescope with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `blescope --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: blescope scan capture.json    # → prioritized findings in seconds (passive, offline)
